name: node-babel-plugin-add-module-exports
title: node-babel-plugin-add-module-exports
summary: Babel plugin for default module.exports like babel version 5
description:
Babel plugin for default module.exports like babel version 5
Babel@6 doesn't export default module.exports any more. This plugin follows
the babel@5 behavior - add the module.exports if only the export default
declaration exists.
.
Babel is a JavaScript compiler to use next generation JavaScript, today.
.
ES2015 and beyond: Babel has support for the latest version of JavaScript
through syntax transformers. These plugins allow you to use new syntax, right
now without waiting for browser support.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-babel-plugin-add-module-exports because Babel plugin for default module.exports like babel version 5.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-babel-plugin-add-module-exports as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-babel-plugin-add-module-exports with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-babel-plugin-add-module-exports --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
